items.title: Sudan’s people subject to ‘relentless’ drone strikes in El Obeid
items.summary: The war in Sudan threatens to spiral further as the strategically-important town of El Obeid continues to face “relentless” drone attacks by advancing paramilitary militias, the UN’s human rights chief, Volker Türk, said on Friday.

items.title: ECB’s Lagarde Will Attend Next Week’s Ecofin Instead of Vujcic
items.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde will be attending next week’s meeting of European Union finance ministers in Brussels instead of her vice president.

items.title: Polish PM warns critical months ahead in face of Russian threat
items.summary: Tusk says Poland is preparing for "various" scenarios after media reports of a planned Russian attack.

items.summary: Officials believe the suspect may not have acted alone and spent days casing out scene of the attack.

items.title: Four Reasons Crimea Has Become Crucial in the Ukraine War
items.summary: Escalating drone strikes, fuel shortages and power cuts in the region Russia annexed are among the factors turning up the heat on President Vladimir V. Putin.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: SQM-Codelco Venture Maps Path to Higher Lithium Output in Chile
items.summary: Chilean mining companies SQM and Codelco are paving the way to boost output at their sprawling lithium partnership by more than 70%, in a long-term bet on battery demand.

items.title: A Long Gulf War Can Starve the World. A Hormuz Transit Deal Could Save Millions.
EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.

items.title: Gaza sports community pledges to continue legacy of killed goalkeeper
items.summary: Slain seeking cooking gas, Gaza goalkeeper Saleem al-Ashqar joins more than 1,000 Palestinian athletes killed in war.

items.title: I had a very substantive conversation with the Prime Minister @MinPres. I am grateful for the readiness to continue supporting our country. I briefed the Prime Minister on the aftermath of Russia’s latest attacks. Today, the rubble clearance operation after the attack overnight into Thursday was completed in Kyiv. That terrorist strike alone claimed the lives of 30 people. My condolences to all the families and loved ones of the victims. It is important that our friends – Ukraine’s friends – are ready to take action to protect lives, and air defense remains one of the key areas of our cooperation with the Netherlands. Today, I also want to thank the Netherlands for its historic decision to host the fully-fledged Special Tribunal for the Crime of Aggression against Ukraine. This decision will make it possible to hold Russia’s highest political and military leadership accountable for the crime of aggression itself, not only for its consequences. We also agreed on the schedule of our next meetings. I thank the Netherlands for its solidarity and support.
items.summary:
